title: using symlink/mount for quadlets
body:
### Issue Description

As described in https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.html which states: (I believe since 4.6)

```
Using symbolic links
Quadlet supports using symbolic links for the base of the search paths and inside them.
```

I'm trying to use our default location /opt/<company-name>/containers where all quadlets reside. This is mounted with fstab to the /etc/containers/systemd

The problem is that once I reboot my server these quadlets are not loaded and requires a systemctl daemon-reload. This is not the case if I place the quadlets directly inside the systemd folder without a mount/symlink.

I saw a previous mention of this problem in: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/20660

Has this since been solved or is there a different solution for this? As we cannot change the location for the systemd rootfull container location like we can do with container storage should this not be a preference?

Why would we need this? We try to benefit as much as possible with all functions podman give us. we use a simple /opt/ location where all our data is backed-up. Container storage, volume location. But cannot change the quadlet location this way.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.create mount point: /opt/test/containers /etc/containers/systemd none bind 0 0
2.place .container in systemd folder
3.start container
4.reboot server
5.container does not start.


### Describe the results you received

Using the mount or symlink towards the systemd folder in /etc/containers/systemd containers do not start at boot.

### Describe the results you expected

Being able to use a symlink/mount as described in the podman docs.

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
As mentioned in your linked issue quadlets must be on the same filesystem as /etc.
That has nothing to do with podman/quadlet but is simply how systemd works.

> Non-essential file systems like /var/ and /home/ are mounted after generators have run.

https://www.freedesktop.org/software/systemd/man/latest/systemd.generator.html

And the same is true even for normal units
